title: Show aggregated telemetry for parent beads including children
description: The TUI detail panel shows telemetry for a single bead only. For parent beads, also show aggregated totals across all children (test, docs, review, correctives). Compute the aggregate on the fly at render time by walking the bead's children in storage and summing their telemetry fields (cost_usd, duration_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens). Display as a separate 'Subtree totals' line in the telemetry section of the detail panel, e.g. 'Subtree: $1.85 total, 12:30 duration, 5 beads'. Also update the list panel telemetry badge to show both own and subtree cost when the bead has children, e.g. '[$0.32 / $1.85]'. Cache the computed aggregates in the existing _detail_cache to avoid recomputing on every render. No bead model or storage changes needed — read children from storage at display time.
acceptance_criteria: Detail panel shows subtree totals for beads with children
List panel badge shows own/subtree cost for parent beads
Aggregates are computed from children's metadata.telemetry
Beads without children show only their own telemetry

handoff_summary.completed: Added _compute_subtree_telemetry() function. Modified _telemetry_badge() with optional subtree_telemetry param. Updated format_detail_panel(), _detail_section_body(), render_detail_panel() with subtree_telemetry param. Added _subtree_cache field and subtree_telemetry_for() method to TuiRuntimeState. Updated refresh() to compute subtree cache. Updated detail_panel_body() cache tuple to include subtree state. Updated Textual: BeadTree._node_label, _populate_bead_tree, _refresh_detail_sections, _update_detail_panel.
handoff_summary.risks: Subtree cache is computed from _beads_cache at refresh time; if a child bead's telemetry changes between refreshes the display may be stale until next refresh (same behavior as own telemetry). The _collect() recursive function could hit Python's default recursion limit for very deep bead trees (unlikely in practice).
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester bead to verify all existing tests pass and new behavior is correct.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — only tui.py was modified and no other in-progress beads claim this file.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — only tui.py was modified and no other in-progress beads claim this file.

execution_history.summary: Implemented aggregated subtree telemetry display in the TUI. Added `_compute_subtree_telemetry()` to walk descendant beads and sum their telemetry. Updated `_telemetry_badge()` to show `[$own / $subtree]` format for parent beads in the list panel. Updated `_detail_section_body()` and `format_detail_panel()` to show a 'Subtree: $X total, M:SS duration, N beads' line in the telemetry section for parent beads. Added `_subtree_cache` to `TuiRuntimeState`, computed in `refresh()`, exposed via `subtree_telemetry_for()`. Updated all Textual app render paths to thread subtree telemetry through. All new parameters are optional with None defaults, preserving full backward compatibility.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
